runtime_boundary: source model only; not mounted runtime evidence

bounds.schedule_semantics: all prefixes over the action alphabet up to max_depth; invalid actions fail closed and preserve state for later prefix exploration

covered_transition_contracts: work enqueue is accepted only while the queue is accepting
begin-teardown transitions accepting queues to draining and refuses duplicate teardown begins
queued work may start while draining but not after final teardown
work completion clears the single modeled running slot
final teardown is rejected while work is queued or running
final teardown transitions to torn-down and increments the owner generation token
teardown callbacks are rejected as sources for normal deferred work

verdict.summary: No explored schedule starts modeled work after final teardown; new work is refused once teardown begins, work starts are refused after final teardown, and final teardown cannot complete while modeled work is queued or running.

live_contract_review.conclusion: No separate mounted C-shim Linux workqueue was found in the reviewed teardown path. The mounted path keeps s_fs_info live through sync_fs and put_super, then calls the Rust engine kill_sb/teardown bridge; this artifact does not replace mounted-kernel runtime validation.
